jokes.text: Hey. Hey, guys, listen. Leslie's gonna walk in and say something that sounds very boring but is actually a really big deal. So when I give the signal, just everybody cheer like crazy.
jokes.setup: Andy sets up elaborate coordination for Leslie's boring announcement
jokes.punchline: The meta-awareness that Leslie's achievements sound boring but matter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - his sweet but dim attempt to help Leslie get recognition for her achievements

jokes.text: This is about people who are moving, Garry. Not everything is about you.
jokes.setup: Multiple people are making moving announcements
jokes.punchline: Tom shuts down Garry's retirement announcement as off-topic
jokes.explanation: Classic Garry mistreatment - he tries to join in but gets dismissed

jokes.text: Also I'm retiring. That's three, Garry.
jokes.setup: Leslie counts bad news in threes, Garry tries to add his retirement
jokes.punchline: Leslie dismissively includes his retirement but still calls him out
jokes.explanation: Continues the Garry dismissal pattern while advancing the plot count

jokes.text: Well, you jinxed us, Garry. Thanks a lot.
jokes.setup: Leslie says they're due for good news, immediately followed by news of mayor's death
jokes.punchline: Leslie blames Garry for the jinx despite her own statement
jokes.explanation: Typical Leslie misdirection of blame away from herself onto Garry

jokes.text: which is just a real cool thing for a public space.
jokes.setup: Mayor's body will lie in state in council chambers
jokes.punchline: Deadpan sarcastic commentary on having a corpse in public space
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan delivery about the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: I wasn't paying attention most of the time. Fortunately, the citizens of Pawnee didn't pay attention either. So if there's credit to take, I will humbly take it. And if there is blame... it's your fault.
jokes.setup: Dead mayor's final video message to the town
jokes.punchline: Takes credit for everything good, blames citizens for everything bad
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical politician farewell - brutally honest about incompetence and blame-shifting

jokes.text: I'm realizing I know nothing about the logistics of how to start an underground dance competition, but someone here will.
jokes.setup: Andy declares they need something to begin - an underground dance competition
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he has no idea how to do it but assumes others do
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - confidently proposing something he knows nothing about

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, you completely disgraced yourself and destroyed an entire town.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions his previous mayoral experience
jokes.punchline: April matter-of-factly describes the Ice Town disaster
jokes.explanation: April's brutal honesty about Ben's past in her characteristic deadpan style

jokes.text: Les-legally Blonde!
jokes.setup: Garry is excited about notarizing a new mayor
jokes.punchline: Awkward pun combining Leslie's name with Legally Blonde
jokes.explanation: Garry's enthusiasm leads to a forced pun, which is perfectly in character

jokes.text: What am I supposed to do with these? Yeah, you're right. I should probably keep them. They're a size 6 1/2. Too small for you anyway.
jokes.setup: Tom gives April men's loafers as a gift
jokes.punchline: Realizes they don't make sense for her, then reveals they're his size anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom self-centeredness disguised as gift-giving

jokes.text: Shia LaBeouf is one hell of a jewelry designer, by the way. Man's really found his calling.
jokes.setup: Tom mentions looking at rings in jewelry stores
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Shia LaBeouf as a jewelry designer
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur delivered matter-of-factly, perfect Tom randomness

jokes.text: What better place to ask a woman to marry you than an old man's public memorial?
jokes.setup: Leslie suggests Tom propose at the memorial
jokes.punchline: Her own recognition that it's completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Leslie's enthusiasm overriding social appropriateness, with self-aware irony

jokes.text: Really makes you consider the 'postulence' of time.
jokes.setup: Andy reflects on the mayor's long tenure
jokes.punchline: Malapropism - 'postulence' instead of 'passage'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy malapropism that sounds almost philosophical

jokes.text: We're born, we survive as long as we're useful, and then we're killed, either by our body's natural decay or by those younger and stronger.
jokes.setup: April responds to Andy's reflection on mortality
jokes.punchline: Brutally clinical description of life and death
jokes.explanation: Peak April darkness delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Before I die, I'm freezing my head like Ted Williams. Wait a thousand years, thaw it out, slap it on a robot, keep on balling.
jokes.setup: Andy responds to April's dark view of death
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan involving cryogenics and robot bodies to 'keep on balling'
jokes.explanation: Andy's childlike optimism about defeating death through absurd means

jokes.text: Was he killed by a younger, stronger barber?
jokes.setup: Learning about Salvatore's death after April's earlier comment
jokes.punchline: Andy applies April's survival theory to barbering
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Andy was actually listening and processing

jokes.text: Walter Gunderson and his wife had an open marriage. It kept him happy. It kept his wife happy. Hi, Evelyn. And it definitely kept me happy.
jokes.setup: Ethel begins what seems like a normal eulogy
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was the mayor's longtime affair partner
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of funeral speech expectations with uncomfortable oversharing

jokes.text: Because for 46 wonderful years, he spent night after night exploring every nook and cranny of my body. Grow up, you prudes. We're all adults.
jokes.setup: Ethel continues her inappropriate eulogy
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual details followed by calling the audience prudes
jokes.explanation: Escalates the inappropriateness while defending it, classic cringe comedy

jokes.text: And not just because he was a dynamite lay.
jokes.setup: Ethel claiming she loved him for more than sex
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining that with another sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern but with diminishing returns, relies on shock value

jokes.text: And not just because he was a dynamite lay. Good night, you animal.
jokes.setup: Ethel's final words about loving the mayor
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to sexual prowess and calls him an animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe ending to an already inappropriate speech

jokes.text: A multiplatform media experience, an action movie with explosive graphics, fast cars, and a hero with one mission-- to propose to his lady. A hero named... Tom Haverford.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks what Tom wants for his proposal
jokes.punchline: Elaborate action movie fantasy ending with his own name as hero
jokes.explanation: Peak Tom grandiosity - turning a proposal into a blockbuster starring himself

jokes.text: Over the past 15 years, I have spent nearly $100,000 at your banner store. My credit card company called me about it. Not to question the charges but to sincerely ask me if I was mentally stable.
jokes.setup: Leslie needs a banner urgently and threatens the banner maker
jokes.punchline: Reveals her obsessive banner spending triggered mental health concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reveals Leslie's obsessiveness while being completely believable

jokes.text: Oh. What was that thing we ran for again? City councilor. I don't know. All right, if you say so. I tell you, it's so hard to remember boring stuff like that.
jokes.setup: Bobby asks how Leslie is doing
jokes.punchline: Can't remember what office they ran for, dismisses it as boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby Newport obliviousness to important political events

jokes.text: Yeah, 'cause I'm getting ready to go on a big trip, to space. That'll be really fun too. Everything in life is always just so fun, right?
jokes.setup: Bobby explains why he can't be mayor
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions space travel like it's a vacation
jokes.explanation: Bobby's wealth making space tourism seem as casual as a weekend trip

jokes.text: I've already been to space. I was the second person to walk on the moon. Buzz Aldrin. Nice to meet you. Nice try. That's the guy from Toy Story.
jokes.setup: Bobby mentions space travel
jokes.punchline: Andy claims to be Buzz Aldrin, Bobby thinks that's the Toy Story character
jokes.explanation: Perfect collision of Andy's fantasy and Bobby's pop culture confusion

jokes.text: I sat in the same chair. Salvatore put the same cape over me. Then he'd always ask me about the same thing... nothing. It was perfect.
jokes.setup: Ron describes his routine with his barber
jokes.punchline: The perfect conversation was about nothing at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - finding perfection in the absence of small talk

jokes.text: As you know, I don't believe in tipping, so I will collect my change from your wife.
jokes.setup: Ron pays for his haircut with a ten-dollar bill
jokes.punchline: Will collect change from the barber's widow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy combining Ron's cheapness with funeral inappropriate timing

jokes.text: What am I supposed to be, some kind or rock star?
jokes.setup: Ron's hair is slightly disheveled after missing his appointment
jokes.punchline: Considers this wildly unkempt like a rock star
jokes.explanation: Ron's conservative appearance standards making slight messiness seem wild

jokes.text: I came up with a backstory for my character. She's married to a French billionaire, and she has a twin sister, identical, and they keep-- Leslie, you have one line.
jokes.setup: Leslie is doing an accent in Tom's proposal video
jokes.punchline: Created elaborate backstory for a one-line role
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-preparation for the tiniest role

jokes.text: Ah, zut alors! - Get 'em, boys! - Freeze! Stunt double.
jokes.setup: Tom's action movie proposal scene
jokes.punchline: Leslie's terrible French accent and immediate stunt double replacement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy and Leslie's overcommitment to the French jewelry store owner character

jokes.text: Lucy, your mission, should you choose to accept it, is to marry me.
jokes.setup: Elaborate action movie proposal setup
jokes.punchline: Mission Impossible reference for marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that fits the action movie theme

jokes.text: No, it wasn't. You just destroyed my case.
jokes.setup: Leslie enthusiastically praises the action sequence
jokes.punchline: Jewelry store owner horrified about his destroyed merchandise
jokes.explanation: Reality check on the consequences of the 'movie' filming

jokes.text: Really? So you were born in the 1990s? Ooh! Mmhmm.
jokes.setup: Bobby reveals he's only 27 years old
jokes.punchline: April and Ben's horrified realization about how young Bobby is
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of adults realizing they're talking to someone born so recently

jokes.text: Money, please! Money, please. My money, please?
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa realizes there's no payment for her performance
jokes.punchline: Increasingly demanding requests for money
jokes.explanation: Mona-Lisa's entitled behavior escalating in typical fashion

jokes.text: And some gross things with those juicy city hall interns. Ga-ga-goink!
jokes.setup: The Douche talks about his mayoral plans
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual comment with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Predictable inappropriate behavior from The Douche character

jokes.text: Ugh. Being nice made me feel terrible.
jokes.setup: April gives Ben genuine advice about being mayor
jokes.punchline: Physical discomfort from acting nice
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - being nice literally hurts her

jokes.text: I'm gonna turn the mayoral office into my oral office. Boom! Thought of it a second ago, had to pop back in. Had to.
jokes.setup: The Douche returns with a new idea
jokes.punchline: Crude pun about oral sex in the mayor's office
jokes.explanation: Cheap wordplay that's in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No, that's not it. Are you in my Frisbee golf league? Oh, yeah. I play for the Super Spinners. Oh, I'm on the Frisbee Champs. I named us.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to place Jeremy Jamm
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're in the same frisbee league with terrible team names
jokes.explanation: Andy's simple naming convention reveals his innocent approach to competition

jokes.text: This is from the Isle of Islay in Scotland. This is as Eastern as my wisdom gets.
jokes.setup: Jamm suggests turning to Eastern wisdom
jokes.punchline: Ron offers Scottish whisky as his version of Eastern wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - dismisses philosophy for pragmatic alcohol solution with geographical precision

jokes.text: Whenever my hair feels it has completed its journey, it simply sheds itself off.
jokes.setup: Jamm asked about who cuts his hair
jokes.punchline: Mystical explanation for natural hair shedding
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Jamm pseudo-spirituality about basic biology

jokes.text: I hate you so much. And I love you, my brother.
jokes.setup: Ron and Jamm's ongoing antagonism
jokes.punchline: Jamm responds to hatred with love
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Ron's directness and Jamm's oblivious positivity

jokes.text: Hey, it's pizza time. And you've lost it.
jokes.setup: April getting excited about being called cool
jokes.punchline: Her immediate return to awkwardness and Tom's recognition of it
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing April's inability to handle compliments gracefully

jokes.text: I'm a cool dudette. Hey, it's pizza time.
jokes.setup: April trying to accept Tom's compliment about being cool
jokes.punchline: Overcorrects with cringey slang
jokes.explanation: April's discomfort with compliments leading to awkward overcompensation

jokes.text: No, no, no, that's just me looking out for you. See, girls get mad horny when you propose. That's why I do it all the time.
jokes.setup: Tom didn't ask for flavored condoms
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's logic about proposals and his frequent proposing
jokes.explanation: Classic Jean-Ralphio inappropriate advice with warped logic

jokes.text: I spent so long trying to forget about Ice Town, but it's part of my life. I mean, it set me on this path. It led me to Pawnee, to my job, to Leslie. I like that Ice Town happened, and I don't want to erase it.
jokes.setup: Ben about to become mayor and erase Ice Town shame
jokes.punchline: Realizes Ice Town was actually a good thing in his life
jokes.explanation: Genuine character growth moment that recontextualizes Ben's biggest shame

jokes.text: Garry Gergich, how would you like to be the next mayor of Pawnee, Indiana? No!
jokes.setup: Ben offers Garry the mayor position
jokes.punchline: Garry's immediate horrified rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff with Garry's characteristic self-doubt

jokes.text: So what's your story again? I mean, like, how married are you? You down to clown? You in that rude mood?
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa hitting on the newly engaged Tom
jokes.punchline: Crude and inappropriate pickup lines
jokes.explanation: Typical Mona-Lisa inappropriate behavior at the worst possible time

jokes.text: I'm writing an electronic opera about Brittany Murphy, and I do the chandelier design for my friend's drag puppet show.
jokes.setup: Ron asks Typhoon what he likes to do
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and bizarre artistic pursuits
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific hipster artist activities that sound completely made up

jokes.text: I'm writing an electronic opera about Brittany Murphy, and I do the chandelier design for my friend's drag puppet show.
jokes.setup: Ron asking what Typhoon does for fun
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd artistic pursuits delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd artistic pretensions, perfectly quotable weirdness

jokes.text: Everyone was on their stupid bikes. I was like, 'Ew.'
jokes.setup: April complaining about Berlin
jokes.punchline: Reducing an entire city to bike annoyance with childish response
jokes.explanation: Perfect April reduction of travel experience to petty complaint

jokes.text: Look, if you didn't want to be on call 24-7 for emergencies, then you should not have gone into the high-stakes world of banner making!
jokes.setup: Leslie needs another banner made urgently
jokes.punchline: Treats banner making like an emergency profession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Leslie's banner obsession into emergency services metaphor

jokes.text: I'm flying!
jokes.setup: Garry being inaugurated as mayor with elaborate celebration
jokes.punchline: Garry's pure joy at unexpected recognition
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Garry's arc from dismissal to ultimate triumph

jokes.text: I'm flying!
jokes.setup: Garry's mayoral inauguration
jokes.punchline: Pure joy expressed as Titanic reference during ceremony
jokes.explanation: Sweet Garry moment - his pure joy at finally being appreciated, even briefly
